payload.user_instructions:
<!-- BEGIN:nextjs-agent-rules -->
# This is NOT the Next.js you know

This version has breaking changes — APIs, conventions, and file structure may all differ from your training data. Read the relevant guide in `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing any code. Heed deprecation notices.
<!-- END:nextjs-agent-rules -->

# Project Notes

## Stack

- Next.js `16.2.4` App Router
- React `19.2.4`
- Prisma with Postgres
- SWR on the client for market, auth, and leaderboard data
- Vercel for production deploys

## Environment Rules

- `DATABASE_URL` is required. `src/lib/prisma.ts` is expected to fail fast if it is missing.
- `AUTH_SECRET` signs the app's HTTP-only session cookies.
- Production must use Vercel environment variables.
- Do not make deploys depend on local `.env` files.
- Keep `.vercelignore` excluding `.env*` unless there is a very specific reason to change that.

## Product Rules

- This is a private friend-group prediction market, not a public Polymarket clone.
- Markets are YES/NO and priced with LMSR logic in `src/lib/pricing.ts`.
- `coins` is liquid balance; `heldCoins` is committed balance on unresolved bets.
- Seed data is for local/dev only. `POST /api/seed` must remain blocked in production.
- Production should use the real database, not demo fallback data.

## Existing Performance Work

- `/leaderboard` uses a client page wrapper, loading shell, and SWR preloading.
- Active market cards preload market detail data and route code.
- Market pages should show loading UI while fetching, not a false "not found" state.
- API routes for markets and leaderboard already set cache headers for Vercel.

When changing navigation or data loading, preserve or improve these behaviors rather than reverting to slower server-only navigation.

## Important Files

- `src/lib/use-data.ts`: SWR hooks, fetcher, preload helpers
- `src/lib/prisma.ts`: Prisma client and env enforcement
- `src/lib/auth.ts`: session cookie auth
- `src/lib/pricing.ts`: LMSR pricing logic
- `src/lib/market-share.ts`: derived market percentages and metadata text
- `src/app/api/markets/route.ts`: market list API
- `src/app/api/markets/[id]/route.ts`: market detail and resolution API
- `src/app/api/bets/route.ts`: bet placement
- `src/app/api/leaderboard/route.ts`: leaderboard API
- `src/app/api/og/market/[id]/route.tsx`: OG image generation

## Deployment Notes

- Standard production deploy command:
  - `npx vercel deploy --prod --yes`
- Main production domain:
  - `https://12polymarket.vercel.app`
- If the wrong deployment is serving, verify the alias on Vercel after deploy.

## Documentation Expectation

If you change runtime behavior, update `README.md` when the setup, deployment flow, API behavior, or operational rules change.

content.text: I think instead of loading all bet at once ( leaderboard, market page ) make it load like batch to optimize the bottleneck of db

payload.message: I think instead of loading all bet at once ( leaderboard, market page ) make it load like batch to optimize the bottleneck of db

I’ll optimize the two DB-heavy paths differently: market pages should paginate activity instead of returning every bet, while leaderboard should aggregate in the database instead of loading all bet rows into JS. I’ll inspect the current data flow and then patch the API/client contracts carefully so existing SWR loading behavior stays intact.

payload.message: The main bottleneck is broader than only the activity list: market summary/detail endpoints currently pull bet rows just to compute volumes/prices, and leaderboard pulls every bet to aggregate stats. I’m refactoring those paths to use DB aggregation plus paginated activity, while still loading the current user’s own bets for the position summary.

content.text: The main bottleneck is broader than only the activity list: market summary/detail endpoints currently pull bet rows just to compute volumes/prices, and leaderboard pulls every bet to aggregate stats. I’m refactoring those paths to use DB aggregation plus paginated activity, while still loading the current user’s own bets for the position summary.

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/burapat/Work/12polymarket-elysia/src/server/routes/markets.ts
@@
 import { Elysia } from 'elysia'
 import type { Prisma } from '@prisma/client'
 import { syncUserBalance } from '@/lib/balances'
 import { prisma } from '@/lib/prisma'
 import { calculateLMSRPrice, DEFAULT_LIQUIDITY } from '@/lib/pricing'
 import { asObject, cacheHeaders, getString } from '@/server/http'
 import { getSessionUser } from '@/server/session'
 
+const MARKET_BET_PAGE_SIZE = 20
+const MAX_MARKET_BET_PAGE_SIZE = 50
+
+type BetVolume = {
+  marketId: string
+  choice: string
+  _sum: {
+    coins: number | null
+  }
+}
+
+type MarketBet = {
+  id: string
+  userName: string
+  coins: number
+  choice: string
+  price: number
+  createdAt: Date
+}
+
+function getBetVolumes(volumes: BetVolume[], marketId?: string) {
+  const scopedVolumes = marketId ? volumes.filter((volume) => volume.marketId === marketId) : volumes
+  const yesVolume = scopedVolumes
+    .filter((volume) => volume.choice === 'YES')
+    .reduce((sum, volume) => sum + (volume._sum.coins ?? 0), 0)
+  const noVolume = scopedVolumes
+    .filter((volume) => volume.choice === 'NO')
+    .reduce((sum, volume) => sum + (volume._sum.coins ?? 0), 0)

payload.name: apply_patch
payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/burapat/Work/12polymarket-elysia/README.md
@@
 - leaderboard and market APIs send cache headers for proxy/CDN caching.
+- market detail loads activity in pages instead of returning every bet at once; current-user position data is fetched separately for the summary card.
+- leaderboard stats are aggregated in Postgres instead of loading every bet row into the API process.
@@
 - market detail avoids false "not found" UI while data is loading
+- market activity uses a paginated "Load more" flow to avoid large detail payloads
 - leaderboard data and market detail data are deduped in SWR
*** End Patch

payload.message:
Implemented and deployed the DB bottleneck optimization.

What changed:
- Market list/detail no longer loads all bets just to calculate volume/prices. It uses Prisma `groupBy`.
- Market detail now returns only the first activity batch, default `20`, with `betsNextCursor`.
- Added `GET /api/markets/:id/bets?cursor=...&limit=...` for paginated activity loading.
- Market page has a `Load more activity` button.
- User position summary still works because the detail endpoint separately returns only the current user’s own bets.
- Leaderboard no longer loads all bet rows into JS. It uses Postgres aggregation via SQL and stored synced balances.

Validation:
- `npm run lint` passed.
- `npm run test:balances` passed.
- `npm run test:pricing` passed.
- `npm run build` passed.
- Production smoke tests passed.

Deployed:
- `https://12polymarket.vercel.app`
- Deployment ID: `dpl_5dBUrUtkgCdNN8jxtbzcXHMHmuqF`

# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
<!-- BEGIN:nextjs-agent-rules -->
# This is NOT the Next.js you know

This version has breaking changes — APIs, conventions, and file structure may all differ from your training data. Read the relevant guide in `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing any code. Heed deprecation notices.
<!-- END:nextjs-agent-rules -->

# Project Notes

## Stack

- Next.js `16.2.4` App Router
- React `19.2.4`
- Prisma with Postgres
- SWR on the client for market, auth, and leaderboard data
- Vercel for production deploys

## Environment Rules

- `DATABASE_URL` is required. `src/lib/prisma.ts` is expected to fail fast if it is missing.
- `AUTH_SECRET` signs the app's HTTP-only session cookies.
- Production must use Vercel environment variables.
- Do not make deploys depend on local `.env` files.
- Keep `.vercelignore` excluding `.env*` unless there is a very specific reason to change that.

## Product Rules

- This is a private friend-group prediction market, not a public Polymarket clone.
- Markets are YES/NO and priced with LMSR logic in `src/lib/pricing.ts`.
- `coins` is liquid balance; `heldCoins` is committed balance on unresolved bets.
- Seed data is for local/dev only. `POST /api/seed` must remain blocked in production.
- Production should use the real database, not demo fallback data.

## Existing Performance Work

- `/leaderboard` uses a client page wrapper, loading shell, and SWR preloading.
- Active market cards preload market detail data and route code.
- Market pages should show loading UI while fetching, not a false "not found" state.
- API routes for markets and leaderboard already set cache headers for Vercel.

When changing navigation or data loading, preserve or improve these behaviors rather than reverting to slower server-only navigation.

## Important Files

- `src/lib/use-data.ts`: SWR hooks, fetcher, preload helpers
- `src/lib/prisma.ts`: Prisma client and env enforcement
- `src/lib/auth.ts`: session cookie auth
- `src/lib/pricing.ts`: LMSR pricing logic
- `src/lib/market-share.ts`: derived market percentages and metadata text
- `src/app/api/markets/route.ts`: market list API
- `src/app/api/markets/[id]/route.ts`: market detail and resolution API
- `src/app/api/bets/route.ts`: bet placement
- `src/app/api/leaderboard/route.ts`: leaderboard API
- `src/app/api/og/market/[id]/route.tsx`: OG image generation

## Deployment Notes

- Standard production deploy command:
  - `npx vercel deploy --prod --yes`
- Main production domain:
  - `https://12polymarket.vercel.app`
- If the wrong deployment is serving, verify the alias on Vercel after deploy.

## Documentation Expectation

If you change runtime behavior, update `README.md` when the setup, deployment flow, API behavior, or operational rules change.

payload.message: Two causes are visible: `/api/auth/me` recalculates balance by scanning that user’s bets every time, and `preloadMarketPageData`/`preloadLeaderboardPageData` explicitly prefetch auth on route hover/navigation. I’m going to make auth reads use the already-synced `User` row and stop preloading auth on every route transition.

payload.name: apply_patch
payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/burapat/Work/12polymarket-elysia/README.md
@@
 - market detail loads activity in pages instead of returning every bet at once; current-user position data is fetched separately for the summary card.
 - leaderboard stats are aggregated in Postgres instead of loading every bet row into the API process.
+- session reads use synced user balances and are not preloaded on route hovers, avoiding repeated bet-ledger scans during navigation.
*** End Patch

# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
<!-- BEGIN:nextjs-agent-rules -->
# This is NOT the Next.js you know

This version has breaking changes — APIs, conventions, and file structure may all differ from your training data. Read the relevant guide in `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing any code. Heed deprecation notices.
<!-- END:nextjs-agent-rules -->

# Project Notes

## Stack

- Next.js `16.2.4` App Router
- React `19.2.4`
- Prisma with Postgres
- SWR on the client for market, auth, and leaderboard data
- Vercel for production deploys

## Environment Rules

- `DATABASE_URL` is required. `src/lib/prisma.ts` is expected to fail fast if it is missing.
- `AUTH_SECRET` signs the app's HTTP-only session cookies.
- Production must use Vercel environment variables.
- Do not make deploys depend on local `.env` files.
- Keep `.vercelignore` excluding `.env*` unless there is a very specific reason to change that.

## Product Rules

- This is a private friend-group prediction market, not a public Polymarket clone.
- Markets are YES/NO and priced with LMSR logic in `src/lib/pricing.ts`.
- `coins` is liquid balance; `heldCoins` is committed balance on unresolved bets.
- Seed data is for local/dev only. `POST /api/seed` must remain blocked in production.
- Production should use the real database, not demo fallback data.

## Existing Performance Work

- `/leaderboard` uses a client page wrapper, loading shell, and SWR preloading.
- Active market cards preload market detail data and route code.
- Market pages should show loading UI while fetching, not a false "not found" state.
- API routes for markets and leaderboard already set cache headers for Vercel.

When changing navigation or data loading, preserve or improve these behaviors rather than reverting to slower server-only navigation.

## Important Files

- `src/lib/use-data.ts`: SWR hooks, fetcher, preload helpers
- `src/lib/prisma.ts`: Prisma client and env enforcement
- `src/lib/auth.ts`: session cookie auth
- `src/lib/pricing.ts`: LMSR pricing logic
- `src/lib/market-share.ts`: derived market percentages and metadata text
- `src/app/api/markets/route.ts`: market list API
- `src/app/api/markets/[id]/route.ts`: market detail and resolution API
- `src/app/api/bets/route.ts`: bet placement
- `src/app/api/leaderboard/route.ts`: leaderboard API
- `src/app/api/og/market/[id]/route.tsx`: OG image generation

## Deployment Notes

- Standard production deploy command:
  - `npx vercel deploy --prod --yes`
- Main production domain:
  - `https://12polymarket.vercel.app`
- If the wrong deployment is serving, verify the alias on Vercel after deploy.

## Documentation Expectation

If you change runtime behavior, update `README.md` when the setup, deployment flow, API behavior, or operational rules change.
